description: Performance baselines for DirectoryCleanerBenchmarks. Thresholds reflect expected results after sequential CalculateFoldersSize fix (commit history below).

2026-03-06_best_old_benchmark.note: Best result with old benchmark (Setup() INSIDE timing, 100 files). Not comparable - included 200x File.WriteAllText.

2026-03-12_regression_old_benchmark.note: Regression from 65a7c65 (Task.Run parallelization). Old benchmark, not comparable.

2026-03-26_baseline.note: New benchmark: setup excluded from timing, 800 files (20 projects). Sequential CalculateFoldersSize fix applied.

workload.note: 20 projects x 2 dirs (bin/Debug, obj/Debug) x 20 files = 800 files total. Setup excluded from timing via [IterationSetup].

CleanAsync.note: 43ms mean observed (2026-03-26, 800 files, setup excluded). Warn at 80ms = ~2x mean. Fail at 130ms catches severe regressions.

regression_analysis.root_cause: 65a7c65 - CalculateFoldersSizeAsync changed from sequential foreach to Task.WhenAll + Task.Run
regression_analysis.mechanism: 20 Task.Run work items for 20 top-level folders; thread pool cold-start overhead (~1ms/thread on Windows) outweighs parallelism benefit for small benchmark workload (10 files/folder)
regression_analysis.fix: Reverted to synchronous sequential CalculateFoldersSize - no thread pool involvement, single-threaded execution

scan_passes.1: GetFoldersToDeleteAsync - directory tree traversal for bin/obj/etc
scan_passes.3: CalculateFoldersSize - GetFiles on each top-level folder before deletion (size reporting only)
scan_passes.note: Pass 3 is redundant for correctness. Future optimization: eliminate pass 3 or make it optional via log level check.
